data.narrative:
Sorcerer is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Austin Tindle, Maxmillion McLaughlin, and Alessandro Vecchi. The company has raised $3.9M to date. The Space & Aerospace sector contains 6 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and a broader peer set including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Given data constraints, only the Stage-Progression method is applicable. Using total raised ($3.9M, rounded to $4M) as a value proxy and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $9.75M. The Peer-Median approach cannot be applied: while 25 same-sector peers exist, insufficient narrow-filter matches (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier) prevent meaningful triangulation. Public-Comp Anchoring is inapplicable due to unavailable revenue data; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S is 42.9x across 5 public tickers, but this cannot be deployed without the target's revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $8M to $11M, centered on $10M. This range reflects ±15% around the Stage-Progression output. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier for Sorcerer's specific subsector and technology maturity, and the absence of comparable private financings at identical stages. Revenue disclosure or additional market-rate Seed rounds in Space & Aerospace would strengthen conviction in any point estimate.
